# cursor-tools

> Single Go binary for Cursor IDE hooks, git hooks, health checks, and memory
> system management. Built for the nfsarch33 agent stack; zero runtime
> dependencies beyond the Go standard library.

## Overview

- Name: cursor-tools
- Purpose: Cursor IDE hook engine (shell guards, file-read sanitisation, MCP gating, post-edit automation), git hooks (commit-msg, pre-push), health-check runner, docs-sync auditor, memory promotion pipeline, and bootstrap installer.
- Language(s): Go
- License: MIT
- Authoritative README: <https://github.com/nfsarch33/cursor-tools#readme>
- Source of truth branch: `main`

## Quick Start

- Install / bootstrap:
  ```
  cd $HOME/cursor-tools
  make build install
  cursor-tools version
  ```
- Canonical CLI verbs:
  - `cursor-tools hook guard-shell` ‚Äî block dangerous shell commands
  - `cursor-tools hook sanitize-read` ‚Äî block secret file reads
  - `cursor-tools hook guard-mcp` ‚Äî gate destructive MCP tools
  - `cursor-tools hook post-edit` ‚Äî format, sync counts, promote
  - `cursor-tools hook housekeeping` ‚Äî log rotation, git sync, promote
  - `cursor-tools githook commit-msg` ‚Äî reject AI attribution, enforce conventional commits
  - `cursor-tools githook pre-push` ‚Äî block direct pushes to main/master
  - `cursor-tools health-check` ‚Äî 33-suite integration health check
  - `cursor-tools docsync check` / `docsync fix` ‚Äî audit and repair docs drift
  - `cursor-tools selftest` ‚Äî hook unit tests (94 assertions)
  - `cursor-tools promote` ‚Äî promote learnings through memory hierarchy
  - `cursor-tools bootstrap` ‚Äî create all symlinks on a fresh machine
- Minimum runtime prerequisites: Go 1.22+, macOS or Linux

## Key Concepts

- Architecture: single binary, all internal packages under `/internal/` (Go compiler enforced, not importable externally)
  - `internal/hookio` ‚Äî Cursor hook JSON stdin/stdout protocol
  - `internal/patterns` ‚Äî pre-compiled regex deny/warn/allow pattern engine
  - `internal/lockfile` ‚Äî cross-platform mkdir and flock locking
  - `internal/logger` ‚Äî structured JSONL logging with rotation
  - `internal/config` ‚Äî platform-aware path configuration
  - `internal/learnings` ‚Äî self-improvement pipeline (parse, merge, digest)
  - `internal/health` ‚Äî multi-suite health check runner
  - `internal/cli` ‚Äî Cobra command definitions
- Testing: `make test` (Ginkgo/Gomega with `-race`), `make test-cover`, `make lint`
- Changelog: see repository commit history

## Runnable Examples

- Build and install: `make build install`
- Run all tests: `make test`
- Run health check: `cursor-tools health-check`
- Run self-test: `cursor-tools selftest`

## Related Repos

- [global-kb](https://github.com/nfsarch33/cursor-global-kb) ‚Äî keeps bootstrap pointers and configuration templates
- [offload-telemetry](https://github.com/nfsarch33/offload-telemetry) ‚Äî shared telemetry module

## Operational Notes

- Issue tracker: <https://github.com/nfsarch33/cursor-tools/issues>
- Rollback: edit `~/.cursor/hooks.json` to point back to legacy `.sh` scripts; old scripts remain in `cursor-config/hooks/` and `cursor-config/bin/`

---

*This file follows the emerging `llms.txt` convention adopted by cloudwego/eino,
Anthropic tooling, and others.*
